summary: A greenfield B2B team workspace, sold per seat: companies sign in via their own SSO, form an org with teams and invites, hold role-gated permissions, organize work into projects and tasks, attach files, keep a compliance audit trail, get notified of activity, and pay per seat.

outcomes.statement: a person can sign in through their company's SSO identity provider, stay signed in while using Hearth, and sign out
an org owner or admin can invite a teammate by email, and that person can accept or decline the invite
owners and admins can do org- and people-management things that ordinary members cannot, and the product enforces that distinction wherever it applies
a member can create a task in a project, assign it, edit it, and mark it complete
a person can attach a file to a task and download it again later
the org has an audit trail of who-did-what that the people running it can review for compliance
the right people are notified, in-app and by email, when things they care about change, and can see a feed of recent activity
an organization can subscribe and pay per seat, and how many people it can have follows from what it is paying for

notes: Greenfield: no existing product, user accounts, billing relationship, or data to migrate.
SSO is the ONLY way in — there is no separate Hearth password.
Role enforcement is required everywhere it matters, not just hidden in the UI.
Billed per seat: the team size a company can have follows from what it is paying for.
